task_description: # Markdown Editor with Live HTML Preview (Reflex) ## Background Build a single-page Reflex application that gives the user a Markdown editing experience with a live HTML preview. The page is split into two columns: an editable Markdown source area on the left, and a live-updating HTML preview on the right. A toolbar above (or beside) the textarea offers `Bold`, `Italic`, and `Code` buttons that wrap the user's current text selection with the appropriate Markdown markers. A counter element shows the live word and character counts of the source. The Markdown-to-HTML conversion must happen on the backend in a cached computed var, and the HTML must be injected into the page via `rx.html`. ## Requirements - A Reflex project at `/home/user/myproject` initialized with `uv` (per the framework's recommended setup) and a Reflex app exposing a page at `/`. - A single Reflex `State` subclass that owns at minimum: - `source: str` — the current Markdown source (controlled value of the textarea). - `selection_start: int` and `selection_end: int` — the cursor's current selection range inside the textarea, updated from a textarea event handler whenever the user changes the selection or types. - A cached computed var (decorated with `@rx.var(cache=True)`) named `preview_html` that converts `self.source` into HTML using the `markdown-it-py` library and returns the HTML string. - Cached computed vars (decorated with `@rx.var(cache=True)`) named `word_count` and `char_count` that return the number of words and the number of characters in `self.source`. - Event handlers `wrap_bold`, `wrap_italic`, and `wrap_code` that mutate `self.source` by wrapping the substring from `selection_start` to `selection_end` with the Markdown markers `**…**`, `*…*`, and `` `…` `` respectively. When the selection is empty (`selection_start == selection_end`), insert the marker pair at the cursor position so the cursor sits between the markers. - The page layout must include the following DOM hooks (these IDs are how the verifier locates each piece): - A `
task_integrations:
- Reflex (target framework)
- markdown-it-py (Python Markdown parser used to render the preview)
truth:
# Verification Plan ## Setup 1. Kill any leftover Reflex processes from prior runs: - `pkill -f 'reflex run' || true` - `pkill -f 'next-server' || true` - `pkill -f 'uvicorn' || true` 2. `cd /home/user/myproject` 3. Start the Reflex app in the background using the start command from the Acceptance Criteria. Wait until both the backend (`http://localhost:8000/ping` returns `pong`) and the frontend (`http://localhost:3000/` returns HTTP 200 with non-empty HTML) are reachable. Use a bounded wait (at most ~180 seconds) since the first run compiles the frontend. 4. Use a headless browser (Playwright/chromium installed in the verifier environment) to drive the page at `http://localhost:3000/`. ## Verification Steps 1. **Static-source checks (no browser required):** - Confirm `/home/user/myproject` exists and contains a Reflex project (e.g. `rxconfig.py` is present). - Confirm that at least one `.py` file under the project contains the regex `@(rx|reflex)\.var\(cache=True\)` AND references either `markdown_it` or `markdown-it-py`. - Confirm that the project's dependency manifest (`pyproject.toml` or `requirements.txt`) declares `markdown-it-py`. 2. **Page loads with required DOM hooks:** - Navigate to `http://localhost:3000/`. - Wait for the textarea with id `md-source` to be visible. Also assert that elements with ids `md-preview`, `md-counter`, `btn-bold`, `btn-italic`, `btn-code` are present in the DOM. 3. **Counter on empty input:** - Clear `#md-source` and dispatch input/change events so state syncs. - Read `#md-counter` text and assert it equals `Words: 0 | Characters: 0`. 4. **Counter on a known input:** - Set `#md-source` value to `the quick brown fox` (4 words, 19 characters) and fire input/change events. - Wait until `#md-counter` text becomes `Words: 4 | Characters: 19`. 5. **Markdown → HTML rendering samples:** for each of the following inputs, set `#md-source` to the input string, fire input/change events, and wait until `#md-preview` innerHTML contains all of the listed substrings. - Input: `# Heading` → expect substring `` inside `#md-preview`. - Input: `**bold**` → expect substring `bold` inside `#md-preview`. - Input: `*italic*` → expect substring `italic` inside `#md-preview`. - Input: `` `code` `` → expect substring `code` inside `#md-preview`. - Input: `- a\n- b` → expect substrings `
    `, `
  • a
    • `, and `
  • b
    • ` inside `#md-preview`. 6. **Toolbar — Bold on selection:** - Set `#md-source` to `hello world`, fire input/change events, then programmatically set `selectionStart=0`, `selectionEnd=5` and dispatch a `select` event on the textarea so the Reflex state captures the selection. - Click `#btn-bold`. - Wait until `#md-source` value equals `**hello** world`. 7. **Toolbar — Italic on selection:** - Set `#md-source` to `hello world`, fire input/change events, then set `selectionStart=6`, `selectionEnd=11` and dispatch a `select` event. - Click `#btn-italic`. - Wait until `#md-source` value equals `hello *world*`. 8. **Toolbar — Code on selection:** - Set `#md-source` to `print(x)`, fire input/change events, then set `selectionStart=0`, `selectionEnd=8` and dispatch a `select` event. - Click `#btn-code`. - Wait until `#md-source` value equals `` `print(x)` ``. 9. **Toolbar — empty selection at cursor:** - Set `#md-source` to `abc`, fire input/change events, then set `selectionStart=selectionEnd=3` and dispatch a `select` event. - Click `#btn-bold`. - Wait until `#md-source` value equals `abc****`. 10. **Caching of `preview_html`:** - Set `#md-source` to `# Heading`, wait for `#md-preview` to contain `Heading` inside an `

    `. Read and store the current `#md-preview` innerHTML. - Without changing the source text, programmatically move the textarea selection (e.g. set `selectionStart=0, selectionEnd=1` and dispatch a `select` event) and wait a short period. Re-read `#md-preview` innerHTML and assert it is exactly equal to the previously stored value (the cached computed var must not produce a different render when only the selection changes). 11. **Teardown:** - Stop the Reflex app processes that were launched during Setup (kill the spawned process group). Confirm that no `reflex run`, `next-server`, or `uvicorn` processes remain attached to port 3000 or 8000.
